creator: bright@hotjobs.com
text:
if you have multiple partitions like so:
1-DOS
2-DOS
3-FreeBSD (root partition here)

then add another freebsd partition like so:
1-DOS
2-FreeBSD (just more space)
3-FreeBSD (root/boot)

then you change partition 2 to a FreeBSD partition you can't boot from partition 3 anymore,
this is on a partition > 1024 i think.

Fix: 

don't know, but you can boot again if you use a install floppie to remove the extra partition.  after you make it ANY kinda of non-freebsd partition, you can boot from it. (ext2fs/dos/nothing)
How-To-Repeat: 
you can use sysinstall to do it, or manually create/change the partition
